item.title: Hackers Target Cybersecurity Firm Outpost24 in 7-Stage Phish
item.description:
In an unsuccessful phishing attack, threat actors leveraged trusted brands and domains to try to redirect a C-suite executive at Outpost24 to give up his credentials.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Less Lucrative Ransomware Market Makes Attackers Alter Methods
item.description:
Ransomware actors are ditching Cobalt Strike in favor of native Windows tools, as payment rates hit record lows and data theft surges.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Clear Communication: The Missing Link in Cybersecurity Success
item.description:
When technical expertise meets clear communication, cybersecurity teams thrive. Learn how to foster trust and collaboration across diverse working groups.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: More Attackers Are Logging In, Not Breaking In
item.description:
Credential theft soared in the second half of 2025, thanks in part to the industrialization of infostealer malware and AI-enabled social engineering.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: 'Claudy Day’ Trio of Flaws Exposes Claude Users to Data Theft
item.description:
A prompt injection vulnerability paired with other flaws can turn a Google search into a full attack chain that could threaten enterprise networks.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: DarkSword: iPhone Exploit Kit Serves Spies & Thieves Alike
item.description:
A sophisticated iOS exploit chain leverages multiple zero-day vulnerabilities and is targeting users in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: EU Sanctions Companies in China, Iran for Cyberattacks
item.description:
These rulings prohibit the entities from entering or doing business in the European Union.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Native Launches With Security Control Plane for Multicloud
item.description:
The cloud security startup's platform translates and enforces security policies across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and Oracle using provider-native controls.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: AI Conundrum: Why MCP Security Can't Be Patched Away
item.description:
RSAC Conference Preview: MCP introduces security risks into LLM environments that are architectural and not easily fixable, researcher says.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Interlock Ransomware Targets Cisco Enterprise Firewalls
item.description:
The ransomware gang, known for double-extortion attacks, had access to a critical Cisco firewall vulnerability weeks before it was publicly disclosed.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Cyber OpSec Fail: Beast Gang Exposes Ransomware Server
item.description:
Files on a central cloud server used by the ransomware group highlight a systematic, aggressive attack on network backups as a key TTP.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Critical Langflow Vulnerability Exploited Hours After Public Disclosure
item.description:
Because attacker-supplied flow data is used in public flows, the bug leads to unauthenticated remote code execution. The post Critical Langflow Vulnerability Exploited Hours After Public Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Allure Security Raises $17 Million for Online Brand Protection
item.description:
The company will invest in expanding its digital brand protection platform and in scaling its go-to-market efforts. The post Allure Security Raises $17 Million for Online Brand Protection appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Navia Data Breach Impacts 2.7 Million
item.description:
Between late December 2025 and mid-January 2026, hackers stole personal and health plan information from Navia’s environment. The post Navia Data Breach Impacts 2.7 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Cape Raises $100 Million for Protection Against Cellular Security Threats
item.description:
Cape offers a privacy-focused mobile virtual network operator (MVNO) service for consumers, enterprises, and governments. The post Cape Raises $100 Million for Protection Against Cellular Security Threats appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: US Confirms Handala Link to Iran Government Amid Takedown of Hackers’ Sites
item.description:
The US has seized several domains used by Handala in cyber-enabled psychological operations. The post US Confirms Handala Link to Iran Government Amid Takedown of Hackers’ Sites appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Eclypsium Raises $25 Million for Device Supply Chain Security
item.description:
The company will use the investment to expand its platform’s capabilities and grow channel partnerships. The post Eclypsium Raises $25 Million for Device Supply Chain Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: In Other News: New Android Safeguards, Operation Alice, UK Toughens Cyber Reporting
item.description:
Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: vulnerabilities found in KVM devices, Claudy Day Claude vulnerabilities, The Gentlemen ransomware group. The post In Other News: New Android Safeguards, Operation Alice, UK Toughens Cyber Reporting appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Critical Quest KACE Vulnerability Potentially Exploited in Attacks
item.description:
The vulnerability is tracked as CVE-2025-32975 and it may have been exploited in attacks against the education sector. The post Critical Quest KACE Vulnerability Potentially Exploited in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: FBI takes down leak sites tied to Iran’s Ministry of Intelligence and Security
item.description:
In a 40-page seizure warrant, the FBI outlined multiple digital campaigns launched by Iran’s Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS) through a variety of online monikers, most recently going by the name “Handala.”
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: California city reports ransomware attack as LA transit agency finds ‘unauthorized activity’
item.description:
Foster City warned that it is possible the hackers obtained public information, urging anyone that has done business with the city to change personal passwords and take measures to protect personal data.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
item.description:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
item.description:
AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and n
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
item.description:
A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
item.description:
The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Russian hackers exploit Zimbra flaw in Ukrainian govt attacks
item.description:
Sergiu Gatlan reports: Hackers part of APT28, a state-backed threat group linked to Russia’s military intelligence service (GRU), are exploiting a Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) vulnerability in attacks targeting Ukrainian government entities. This high-severity security flaw (tracked as CVE-2025-66376 and patched in early November) stems from a stored cross-site scripting (XSS) that unauthenticated attackers can exploit to gain remote... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: UMMC continues investigating cyberattack and recovering from impact.
item.description:
In February, the University of Mississippi Medical Center (UMMC) announced it was closing dozens of its clinics statewide after a cyberattack disrupted multiple IT systems and cut off access to its Epic EMR platform, which contained patients’ medical records. The attack resulted in a nine-day service suspension, during which most outpatient clinics statewide were closed,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Justice Department Disrupts Iranian Cyber Enabled Psychological Operations
item.description:
A Court-Authorized Domain Seizure Removes Four Websites Facilitating the Islamic Republic of Iran’s Ministry of Intelligence and Security’s Hacking Efforts Tied to Psychological Operations and Transnational Repression WASHINGTON – Today, the Justice Department announced the seizure of four domains as part of an ongoing effort to disrupt hacking and transnational repression schemes conducted by the Islamic Republic... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: North Carolina tech worker found guilty of insider attack netting $2.5M ransom
item.description:
Matt Kapko reports: A 27-year-old North Carolina man was found guilty of six counts of extortion for a series of crimes he committed while working as a data analyst contractor for a D.C.-based international technology company, the Justice Department said Thursday. Cameron Nicholas Curry, also known as “Loot,” stole a trove of corporate data, including sensitive... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: University College of Dublin staff member due in court over accessing student data
item.description:
Paul Reynolds provides today’s reminder of the insider threat. This one involves a univeristy in Dublin, Ireland. A UCD staff member is due in court this morning charged in connection with unlawfully accessing student data at the college. The man, who is in his 50s, was arrested this morning following an investigation by the Garda... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Global cybercrime crackdown: over 373,000 dark web sites shut down
item.description:
From Europol, some impressive results: On 9 March 2026, a global operation led by German authorities and supported by Europol was launched against one of the largest networks of fraudulent platforms in the dark web. The investigation began in mid-2021 against the dark web platform “Alice with Violence CP”. During the investigation, authorities discovered that... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Jaguar Land Rover’s cyber bailout sets worrying precedent, watchdog warns
item.description:
Carly Page reports: The UK’s cyber watchdog has warned that the government’s £1.5 billion bailout of Jaguar Land Rover (JLR) risks setting a troubling precedent for how Britain handles major cyber crises. Speaking at an event marking the Cyber Monitoring Centre’s (CMC) first operational year, Ciaran Martin, chair of the CMC’s technical committee and a distinguished fellow... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Strengthening Cybersecurity in Canada’s Municipal Sector: A Verified Analysis
item.description:
From the Nonprofit Tech Support: When the City of Hamilton suffered a ransomware attack on February 25, 2024, it marked a sobering milestone in Canadian municipal cybersecurity. The attack crippled roughly 80% of Hamilton’s network, impacting services from business licensing to the fire department’s records. Attackers demanded an $18.5 million ransom, which the city refused... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Cyber OpSec Fail: Beast Gang Exposes Ransomware Server
item.description:
Robert Lemos writes: An open server hosted on a German cloud provider’s systems has been discovered, containing the entire toolset of a member of the Beast ransomware group. The find exposes the tactics, techniques, and procedures of the threat actor, but also reveals that Beast shares many of those TTPs with other ransomware gangs. According... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Weill Cornell Medicine discloses an insider data breach
item.description:
On February 23, HHS received a breach submission from Weill Cornell Medicine in New York. The submission reported that 516 patients were affected by an incident involving Unauthorized Access/Disclosure of data in Electronic Medical Records (EMR). DataBreaches emailed the hospital to request an explanation and received the following statement from a spokesperson: After thorough investigation,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: PHI Exposed in Data Breaches at Cedar Valley Services; Community Nurse; Health Dimensions Group
item.description:
Data breaches have recently been reported by Cedar Valley Services and Health Dimensions Group in Minnesota, and Community Nurse in […] The post PHI Exposed in Data Breaches at Cedar Valley Services; Community Nurse; Health Dimensions Group appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Delta Medical Systems Notifies Patients About July 2025 Cyberattack
item.description:
Data breaches have recently been announced by Delta Medical Systems in Wisconsin, Ansell Healthcare Products in New Jersey, and FuturHealth […] The post Delta Medical Systems Notifies Patients About July 2025 Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Trinity Health & UPMC Notify Patients About Potential Unauthorized Data Access via HIE
item.description:
Trinity Health and the University of Pittsburgh Medical Center are notifying patients about potential unauthorized access to patient data by […] The post Trinity Health & UPMC Notify Patients About Potential Unauthorized Data Access via HIE appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Free Webinar: HIPAA Email Security 101: PHI, Encryption, and What’s Required
item.description:
According to the Paubox 2026 Healthcare Email Security Report, in 2025, 170 email-related data breaches were reported to the HHS’ […] The post Free Webinar: HIPAA Email Security 101: PHI, Encryption, and What’s Required appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: CISA Advises U.S. Organizations to Harden Microsoft Intune Following Stryker Data Wiping Attack
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is urging U.S. organizations to strengthen administrative controls for the Intune endpoint […] The post CISA Advises U.S. Organizations to Harden Microsoft Intune Following Stryker Data Wiping Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Final Rule Implementing HIPAA Security Rule Updates Edges Closer
item.description:
The HIPAA Security Rule update proposed by OCR in the final days of the Biden administration is only two months […] The post Final Rule Implementing HIPAA Security Rule Updates Edges Closer appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Essen Medical Associates Agree to $4 Million Settlement to Resolve Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit
item.description:
Essen Medical Associates has agreed to pay $4,000,000 to resolve class action litigation over a March 2023 cyberattack and data […] The post Essen Medical Associates Agree to $4 Million Settlement to Resolve Class Action Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Navia Benefit Solutions Discloses Data Breach Affecting 2.7 Million Individuals
item.description:
Over a three-week period between December 2025 and January 2026, hackers had access to the network of a Washington-based employee […] The post Navia Benefit Solutions Discloses Data Breach Affecting 2.7 Million Individuals appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Bitrefill blames North Korean Lazarus group for cyberattack
item.description:
Crypto-powered gift card store Bitrefill says that the attack it suffered at the beginning of the month was likely perpetrated by North Korean hackers of the Bluenoroff group. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Navia discloses data breach impacting 2.7 million people
item.description:
Navia Benefit Solutions, Inc. (Navia) is informing nearly 2.7 million individuals of a data breach that exposed their sensitive information to attackers. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: How CISOs Can Survive the Era of Geopolitical Cyberattacks
item.description:
Geopolitical tensions are driving destructive cyberattacks designed to disrupt operations, not demand ransom. CISOs must limit lateral movement and contain breaches to reduce the impact of wiper campaigns. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: FBI links Signal phishing attacks to Russian intelligence services
item.description:
The FBI has issued a public service announcement warning that Russian intelligence-linked threat actors are actively targeting users of encrypted messaging apps such as Signal and WhatsApp in phishing campaigns that have already compromised thousands of accounts. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Microsoft Azure Monitor alerts abused for callback phishing attacks
item.description:
Microsoft Azure Monitor alerts are being abused to send callback phishing emails that impersonate warnings from the Microsoft Security Team about unauthorized charges on your account. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Trivy vulnerability scanner breach pushed infostealer via GitHub Actions
item.description:
The Trivy vulnerability scanner was compromised in a supply-chain attack by threat actors known as TeamPCP, which distributed credential-stealing malware through official releases and GitHub Actions. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News
